title: Eragrostis ciliata
intro: Eragrostis ciliata, also known as Weeping Lovegrass, is a perennial grass species native to tropical Asia, characterized by its slender stems and drooping panicles. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and fever, due to its potential antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Eragrostis ciliata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Eragrostis ciliata is commonly prepared as a decoction or tea, and is also used in traditional formulations, such as infusions and tonics, to support immune function and alleviate respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Eragrostis ciliata is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. This genus, Eragrostis, is commonly known as love grass or crow's tail grass. The species Eragrostis ciliata falls under the order Poales, which includes various grasses and sedges. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Eragrostis ciliata is often found growing in areas with moist or wet soils.
